title: rosa communis var. spuria
intro: Rosa communis var. spuria, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The rose hips, in particular, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate various health issues, such as colds, fever, and digestive problems. Rosa communis var. spuria can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used as a natural remedy for its health benefits, including improving cardiovascular health, boosting the immune system, and supporting the digestive system.
